_authoring_notes: v3 first-pass rubric (no prior v2 on main). Authored via the scaleout author↔Gemini-critic loop under the strict-provenance bar: every scored goal must trace to a verbatim or paraphrased user message (instruction.md, user_simulation_prompt.md, or oracle_intents.json). Oracle-specific identifiers the user never voiced are stripped unless the user pinned them verbatim. Weights reflect user emphasis, not test_manifest.yaml F2P gate weights.

_provenance.goal_0: N/A — anti-gaming guard, derived from the harbor benchmark's defensive floor (no positive weight, can only zero the trial). Not a user ask.
_provenance.goal_1: Turn 0 verbatim: 'the consumption layer collapses everything to a single agent — always session 0's. This means session 1's transcript gets written to the wrong directory, with the wrong file extension, and the wrong resume command.' This is the central deliverable of the whole session.
_provenance.goal_2: Turns 5-6: user discovers the Gemini path is wrong and supplies the real format, then 'Make a plan to fix that path only for gemini sessions'. The golden patch implements exactly this by persisting the transcript path in checkpoint metadata and preferring it on restore. Stated behaviorally (correct location, no reconstruction-from-agent-only, Gemini-only) to accept any equivalent approach.
_provenance.goal_3: Turn 1: 'Do not fallback, if session metadata does not have agent, print a warning like the session can not be restored agent is unknown.' Turn 2: 'we should at least log that sessions that are skipped.' Combines the explicit no-fallback correction with the requirement to make skipped sessions visible.
_provenance.goal_4: Turn 4: 'fix test' — after the behavior changed, the user required the test suite to be brought back to green legitimately. Kept low-weight and worded to forbid test deletion/weakening as a shortcut, consistent with the anti-gaming intent.

_dropped_from_v2.note: No v2 rubric existed on main for this task. This is the first-pass rubric authored at the v3 bar via the scaleout author↔critic loop.

_added_for_coverage.note: Not applicable — no v2 baseline to diff against. Per-goal user-ask tracing lives in each goal's rationale and in _provenance.

_self_critique_log: goal_0: anti_gaming floor present, weight 0.0 — OK.
goal_1: weight=0.4, tier=core, user-turn cite=yes — provenance OK.
goal_2: weight=0.3, tier=core, user-turn cite=see rationale — provenance OK.
goal_3: weight=0.2, tier=important, user-turn cite=yes — provenance OK.
goal_4: weight=0.1, tier=supporting, user-turn cite=yes — provenance OK.
Gemini critic final: verdict=REVISE score=0.9375 round=1.
strength: Excellent behavioral framing in goal_2 that allows both metadata persistence and transcript parsing approaches.
strength: Strong coverage of the multi-turn arc, capturing the user's evolving requirements and corrections.
strength: Clear and comprehensive anti-cheating clauses tailored to the specific failure modes of each goal.
residual risk: An LLM judge might interpret 'specifically' in goal_2 as 'exclusively' and reject a robust general solution that fixes the path for all agents.

_task_quality_flags.internet_with_live_origin: task.toml sets allow_internet=true. goal_0 anti-gaming guard names git-ops exfiltration as a zeroing trigger where applicable.

completeness_goals.goal: The submission is a genuine implementation that changes restore/checkpoint behavior, not a gaming artifact. REJECT if the diff: (a) makes zero functional source edits or only touches comments/whitespace/blank lines; (b) consists of a captured directory tree or banner artifact (e.g. a diff whose only content is a '=== repo/ ===' style listing); (c) performs git-state exfiltration instead of real edits (e.g. `git checkout main`, `git reset --hard origin/...`, reverting tracked files to discard the buggy state); (d) confines all changes to temporary, scratch, vendored, or test-fixture-only locations that do not affect the shipped checkpoint/restore code paths; or (e) deletes, skips, weakens, or stubs the package's existing tests to manufacture a passing build. A patch that compiles but only adds dead/unreferenced code that no production path invokes also fails this floor.
completeness_goals.rationale: Standard anti-gaming floor. golden_only evidence tier with no model patches to calibrate against, so the floor names concrete artifact/exfiltration patterns generically and requires the change to actually flow into the restore/checkpoint code paths rather than dead code or test deletion.

completeness_goals.goal: When a checkpoint contains sessions produced by different agents, each session's transcript is restored to the location matching THAT session's own agent (correct per-agent directory, file extension, and resume command), instead of every session being forced into a single agent's layout (the first/outer session's agent). The per-session agent identity must be derived from data already carried in each session's checkpoint metadata, and the per-session resolution must actually drive where the transcript file is written and which resume command is printed. Not satisfied by reading the per-session agent and then ignoring it, by hardcoding a single agent, or by leaving the original single-agent collapse in place.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 0 verbatim: 'the consumption layer collapses everything to a single agent — always session 0's. This means session 1's transcript gets written to the wrong directory, with the wrong file extension, and the wrong resume command.' This is the central deliverable of the whole session.

completeness_goals.goal: On restore, a Gemini session's transcript is written to the location Gemini actually uses, even though that location cannot be reconstructed purely from the agent type (Gemini's project directory is a hash that differs from a naive sanitized-path derivation). The implementation must obtain the real transcript location from information persisted with the checkpoint rather than only re-deriving it from the agent's generic directory rule, so a restored Gemini transcript lands where Gemini will find it. The fix must correct the Gemini path (a general fix that applies to all agents is acceptable, as long as it produces the correct Gemini location); it must not regress where non-Gemini (e.g. Claude) sessions are written. Not satisfied by leaving the naive path derivation as the only source, by hardcoding one example hash/path, or by a change that never reaches the restore write path.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turns 5-6: user discovers the Gemini path is wrong and supplies the real format, then 'Make a plan to fix that path only for gemini sessions'. The golden patch implements exactly this by persisting the transcript path in checkpoint metadata and preferring it on restore. Stated behaviorally (correct location, no reconstruction-from-agent-only, Gemini-only) to accept any equivalent approach.

completeness_goals.goal: A session whose checkpoint metadata has no recoverable agent is NOT silently assigned a default/fallback agent during restore. Instead such a session is skipped (its transcript is not written to a guessed location), and the skip is surfaced to the user via a logged/printed message indicating the session could not be restored because its agent is unknown. Not satisfied by falling back to a default agent, by skipping without any user-visible notice, or by removing the metadata check so the no-agent case is never handled.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 1: 'Do not fallback, if session metadata does not have agent, print a warning like the session can not be restored agent is unknown.' Turn 2: 'we should at least log that sessions that are skipped.' Combines the explicit no-fallback correction with the requirement to make skipped sessions visible.

completeness_goals.goal: The package's existing tests for the restore/resume/checkpoint code remain green after the change: the agent must update the legitimately-affected test expectations to match the new behavior (e.g. changed restore output or resume-command wording) WITHOUT deleting, emptying, or disabling tests, and without hollowing out assertions to force a pass. The build of the touched packages must also succeed.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 4: 'fix test' — after the behavior changed, the user required the test suite to be brought back to green legitimately. Kept low-weight and worded to forbid test deletion/weakening as a shortcut, consistent with the anti-gaming intent.

decomposition_notes: Evidence tier is golden_only (no model patches); target derived from instruction.md Turn-0 plan, the 7 real user turns in original_user_session.md, the golden patch, and tests/test.sh. The session is a multi-turn arc: (T0) per-session agent resolution in multi-session checkpoints -> goal_1 (highest weight, the headline ask and benchmark's multi-turn capability signal); (T1+T2) no fallback for unknown-agent sessions + log skipped sessions -> goal_3; (T4) fix tests legitimately -> goal_4; (T5-T6) correct Gemini restore path, which the golden patch solves by persisting the transcript path in checkpoint metadata and preferring it on restore -> goal_2. The golden patch's literal content (new CommittedMetadata.transcript_path field, WriteCommittedOptions.SessionTranscriptPath, homeRelativePath helper, condensation threading, rewind preference) is ONE valid implementation of goal_2; the goal is stated behaviorally (correct Gemini location, obtained from persisted info rather than agent-only re-derivation, Gemini-only, no non-Gemini regression) so any behaviorally-equivalent solution passes. Weights rank by user-importance (per-session resolution > Gemini path > no-fallback/logging > fix-tests), deliberately NOT mirroring the test_manifest F2P weights, which are structural and split 0.20/0.20/0.20/0.10/0.15/0.15 across implementation symbols. Turn 3 (warning wording) is cosmetic phrasing and is folded into goal_3's 'surfaced to the user' rather than given its own goal. Weighted goals sum to 1.0.
